title: Research: Monoliths vs Microservices Background
description:
Write a comprehensive background-and-context document covering:
- Definition and characteristics of monolithic architecture
- Definition and characteristics of microservices architecture
- Key tradeoffs across scalability, deployment, complexity, organizational impact, testing, and operational overhead
- Real-world examples and patterns for each approach
- Technology stack considerations

This research will form the foundation for the analysis phase.

title: Analysis: Evaluate and Rank Architectural Tradeoffs
description:
Critically evaluate the research and produce an analysis document that:
- Identifies the top tradeoffs between monoliths and microservices
- Ranks them by impact and importance
- Evaluates pros/cons for different contexts (startup, enterprise, scale, team size, etc.)
- Provides decision criteria for teams choosing between the two
- Highlights when each approach is most appropriate

This will be the analytical backbone of the final brief.

title: Write: Produce Polished Brief
description:
Synthesize research and analysis into a tight, well-structured brief (approx. 1500-2000 words) on architectural tradeoffs. The brief should:
- Open with a clear thesis on when to choose each approach
- Present the key tradeoffs in an organized, executive-friendly format
- Use tables, bullet points, or visual structure as appropriate
- Include decision framework for practitioners
- Conclude with actionable recommendations

Target audience: architects and engineering leaders making architectural decisions.
